texto: When people quit talking to God, they quit talking to one another. And people who quit talking to God soon get very lonely and depressed. texto: You win over people just like you win over a dog. You see a dog passing down the street with an old bone in his mouth. You don't grab the bone from him and tell him it's not good for him. He'll growl at you. It's the only thing he has. But you throw a big fat lamb chop in front of him, and he's going to drop that bone and pick up the lamb chop, his tail wagging to beat the band. And you've got a friend. Instead of going around grabbing bones from people... I'm going to throw them some lamb chops. Something with real meat and life in it. I'm going to tell them about New Beginnings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Wilkerson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You win over people just like you win over a dog. You see a dog passing down the street with an o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, christ, grace pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e grace.

texto: A suburban pastor maintained services appropriate for his respected, professional parish. His father, an excitable traveling evangelist, visited and challenged the congregation to confront pride and sing out loudly with the windows open. The next day, the pastor’s banker mentioned overhearing, and he was sheepish. The buttoned-up banker said, though, that the neighborhood had been WAITING TO HEAR the church live out the joy they claimed.
